question.text: For a commodity future to be eligible as an index constituent, it must have been in existence for at least:

answer.text: 12 months and traded on 90% of trading days during this period
6 months and traded on 80% of trading days
24 months and traded on 95% of trading days
3 months and traded on 75% of trading days

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per SEBI guidelines, a constituent's commodity futures must have been in existence for at least the last 12 months and traded on 90% of trading days during these 12 months. **The Trap:** All other combinations of months and percentages are incorrect — 12 months and 90% are the specific SEBI requirements. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII has similar eligibility criteria for equity index constituents. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This 12-month/90% rule ensures only established, liquid commodities enter the index — protecting index investors from illiquid or newly launched contracts.
